ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER ITS COUNSEL'S SEPTEMBER 17 LETTER THAT METALCLAD IS NO LONGER SEEKING ADJUDICATION OF THE FAILURE TO WARN CLAIMS. METALCLAD'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE EXCLUDING FAILURE TO WARN) AND 3 (STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT) AND TAKEN OF CALENDAR AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUES PER METALCLAD'S REQUEST. THE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT METALCLAD IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR ON THE NON-FAILURE TO WARN NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CLAIMS BASED ON THE GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE. ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO MONDAY SEPTEMBER 27, 2010 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 220. THE COURT DID RECEIVE NOTIFICATIONS FROM BOTH SIDES TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING ON SEPTEMBER 22. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-23-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 220

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa Oberg, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4127), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Peter Bovinich Jr. (#275067) and William Glover (#456641)

RULING:
The hearing on defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motions for summary judgment in #275067 (Bovinich) and #456641 (Glover) were heard together.
Argued and the Court took defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motions for summary judgment in #275067 (Bovinich) and #456641 (Glover) under submission. Per Diem Fee accessed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2010 Continued To Sep-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is off calendar per its counsel's September 17 letter that Metalclad is no longer seeking adjudication of the failure to warn claims. Metalclad's alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 (negligence excluding failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and taken of calendar as to the remaining issues per Metalclad's request. The admissible evidence establishes that Metalclad is entitled to judgment in its favor on the non-failure to warn negligence and strict liability claims based on the government contractor defense. On the court's own motion the hearing is continued to Monday September 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220. The Court did receive notifications from both sides to contest the tentative ruling on September 22. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
